title: String length counts encoding units, not visible characters
rule: A string length often counts encoding units rather than user-visible characters: JS .length counts UTF-16 code units, so an emoji or a combining accent can be length 2 or more and a family emoji 8, while the byte length differs again. Do not use length for character counts, truncation, or column limits; iterate by code point or grapheme instead.
triggers: string length counts more than the visible characters
an emoji has length 2 in JavaScript
truncating a string by length splits a character
length does not match the number of characters
